body:
## Summary
- Add `--focus-progress-bar-accessibility` to `scripts/validate-content.js` so ProgressBar accessibility parity can run without the full validator.
- Point `tests/content-progress-bar-accessibility-parity.test.js` and its mutation fixtures at the focused flag.
- Add a routing guard in `tests/content-test-script-routing.test.js` so the ProgressBar parity suite cannot silently fall back to full content validation.

## Validation
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node scripts/validate-content.js --focus-progress-bar-accessibility`
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --test tests/content-progress-bar-accessibility-parity.test.js tests/content-test-script-routing.test.js --test-name-pattern "ProgressBar|progress bar|focus-progress-bar|adapter|duplicate"`
- `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`

## Known existing blocker
- Full `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' npm run validate:content` remains blocked by existing practice/progress validator drift: visible completed-question ids, persisted progress JSON read/normalization checks, and the `PracticeSessionState` `startSession` field contract.
title: Add focused ProgressBar accessibility gate
